name: OPEC+
climate: Extremely diverse: arid desert, tropical, continental, subarctic — spans from equatorial Africa to Siberia

neighbors.name: OPEC
neighbors.relevance: Core bloc within OPEC+; 13 OPEC members form the foundation of the alliance

neighbors.name: Gulf Cooperation Council
neighbors.relevance: GCC states hold most OPEC+ spare capacity and swing production capability

neighbors.name: CIS
neighbors.relevance: Russia, Kazakhstan, Azerbaijan are OPEC+ non-OPEC members from the post-Soviet space

neighbors.name: ASEAN
neighbors.relevance: Malaysia and Brunei are OPEC+ members; Southeast Asia is a growing oil demand center

neighbors.name: Persian Gulf
neighbors.relevance: Gulf producers dominate OPEC+ output and policy direction

links.description: All 13 OPEC members are part of OPEC+; OPEC forms the core decision-making group
GCC states lead OPEC+ policy; Saudi Arabia sets baseline for production cuts
Russia is the most important non-OPEC member; Saudi-Russia coordination defines OPEC+ effectiveness
OPEC+ production decisions directly impact G20 economies and inflation
Gulf OPEC+ members depend on Hormuz; Russia uses alternative pipelines and Arctic routes
US shale production competes directly with OPEC+ for market share
Several OPEC+ members are BRICS members; coordinating energy and economic policy
Russia's Arctic LNG exports provide alternative supply outside traditional OPEC+ chokepoints
